title: Expand contaminant library (radionuclides, chromium-6, DBP subspecies), expert-reviewed
context: After the core 8-contaminant library is established in M1, M4 expands coverage to additional regulated contaminants with evolving regulatory status. Radionuclides, chromium-6, and disinfection byproduct subspecies are significant in some communities and have regulatory-evolution histories that must be flagged for re-review. All new entries must follow the same expert-review, no-verdict, and no-self-approval workflow as the core library.
objective: Expand the contaminant library with entries for radionuclides (combined radium, gross alpha/beta, uranium), chromium-6 (hexavalent chromium), and additional disinfection byproduct subspecies beyond TTHM and HAA5, each: (1) plain-language and sourced to EPA/WHO with retrieval dates; (2) carrying no verdict; (3) flagging any regulatory-evolution items for re-review; (4) expert-signed-off by an approver distinct from the author with a PR-tied review-log entry.
acceptanceCriteria: New entries added for radionuclides (combined radium, gross alpha/beta, uranium), chromium-6, and additional DBP subspecies
Every figure and claim sourced to EPA/WHO with retrieval dates and license; no verbatim copying
Every new entry carries no safety verdict, grade, ranking, or product recommendation
Regulatory-evolution items (pending/recent rule changes) flagged for re-review with a named owner
Every entry expert-signed-off by a qualified approver distinct from the author; PR-tied review-log entry committed for each
output: Document: expanded contaminant library entries in content/contaminants/ with expert review-log entries for each new entry.
